name: Winter Vegetable Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 1 turnip, peeled, cut into bite-size wedges
6 tablespoons butter or margarine, chilled, cut into small pieces
3/4 cup cream (or use half&half or fat free evaporated milk or soy milk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the turnip, potato, celery root, onion, carrots, and parsley in a 2-inch deep ovenproof baking dish (there should be about 6 cups of vegetables).
2. In a small mixing bowl, blend the broth with the cornstarch, pour over the vegetables, and mix well.
3. Add salt and pepper to taste and mix to blend.
4. Dot the top of the vegetables with the butter.
5. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
6. Make the cobbler dough: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt in a large mixing bowl and stir with a fork to blend.
7. Drop the pieces of chilled butter into the flour mixture and rub quickly with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
8. Using a fork, slowly stir in the cream until roughly mixed.
9. Gather the dough into a shaggy mass and knead five or six times.
10. Roll the dough on a lightly floured board to the size of the top of the baking dish. The dough should be about 1/4-inch thick.
11. Place the dough on top of the vegetables.
12. Bake for 45-55 minutes until vegetables are cooked through and crust is browned.
13. Test vegetables for doneness with a knife tip or skewer.
14. Remove from oven and let sit about 10 minutes.
